title: Learned next-compound transition priors with hierarchical smoothing
summary: The best code already checks whether fresh soft/medium/hard can finish, but teams do not choose those compounds uniformly.
rationale: The best code already checks whether fresh soft/medium/hard can finish, but teams do not choose those compounds uniformly. Strategy guides emphasize track-specific tyre choice, warm-up, and offset tradeoffs, and the dry-race rule means the preferred next compound depends on what has already been used.
implementation_hint: Recover actual post-stop compounds from training sequences, then create out-of-fold priors such as `P(next_compound | current_compound, stint, laps_remaining_bin, race, year, wet_dry, slicks_used, prior_pits)`, smoothed hierarchically from race-year to race to global. Convert them into features like `prob_switch_to_hard`, `prob_same_compound_repeat`, `transition_entropy`, and `expected_finish_margin_under_likely_next_compound`.
expected_effect: Adds track- and regime-specific strategic preference information without external labels, especially for separating one-stop extension rows from genuine stop-call rows.
risk: Sparse transition tables can overfit and same-race leakage is a real trap; build priors strictly out-of-fold and shrink aggressively.
